item.title: Using Push data to quantify identity vulnerabilities
item.description: Using Push data to calculate how many vulnerable identities the average organization has, and how they lead to different methods of account takeover.

item.title: How Push detects and blocks phishing attacks in the browser
item.description: How Push detects and blocks phishing attempts in the browser – explained in less than two minutes.

item.title: How attackers defeat detections based on page signatures
item.description: How attackers are breaking detection signatures designed to identify phishing sites impersonating real login pages.

item.title: Cross-IdP impersonation: hijacking SSO using fraudulent IdPs
item.description: Cross-IdP impersonation is a method of hijacking SSO to access downstream apps — without needing to compromise accounts on your company’s main IdP.

item.title: Combining Verification Phishing and Cross-IdP Impersonation
item.description: How phishing for email verification can be combined with cross-IdP impersonation to gain direct access to downstream SaaS and bypass hardened IdP accounts.

item.title: Using Push to automate SSO password resets
item.description: Using Push to automate password resets for your most critical identities when a password vulnerability is detected.

item.title: Public breaches from identity attacks in 2024
item.description: Reviewing public breaches that stemmed from identity attacks in 2024.

item.title: How to securely deploy browser extensions to the web store
item.description: How extension developers can improve their security controls to prevent extension compromise.

item.title: Using Push to enforce MFA on third-party apps in the browser
item.description: Using Push to enforce MFA on third-party apps in the browser — even where MFA enforcement isn't supported by the app itself.

item.title: How Computer-Using Agents can be leveraged in cyber attacks
item.description: CUAs are a new type of AI agent that drives your browser/OS for you, enabling effortless automation of web tasks — including those performed by attackers.

item.title: Using OpenAI Operator to automate credential stuffing
item.description: Credential stuffing attacks had a huge impact in 2024. But things could be dialled up even further with Computer-Using Agents like OpenAI Operator.

item.title: Using CUAs like Operator to automate identity attacks
item.description: We're back with part 2 of our research into OpenAI Operator to share our findings on how it can be used to automate identity attacks.

item.title: Dissecting a recent MailChimp phishing attack
item.description: HIBP creator and well-known security person Troy Hunt recently blogged about a phish he fell for. Here’s what it tells us about how phishing is evolving.

item.title: Analyzing two different forms of consent phishing
item.description: Consent phishing is where attackers trick users into authorizing access for malicious OAuth apps. Here's how attackers are using this technique in the wild.

item.title: Navigating the 2025 HIPAA Security Rule changes
item.description: The HIPAA Security Rule is getting a long-overdue facelift in 2025. Here's our quick overview of the key changes and how Push can help you to be compliant.

item.title: Integrate Cribl and Push Security
item.description: We’re thrilled to announce our partnership with Cribl to make it much easier to snapshot, transform, and query Push telemetry.

item.title: Introducing the Employee Identity Verification Code feature
item.description: Push's new Employee Identity Verification Codes feature is a simple way for your help desk to confirm they’re talking to someone from your organization.

item.title: How browser-level controls change the fight against phishing
item.description: Attackers are routinely defeating conventional email, network, and endpoint-based security controls. Here's how browser controls can level the playing field.

item.title: App-Specific Password phishing and how to prevent it
item.description: How App-Specific Password phishing is being used in the wild to bypass phishing-resistant authentication controls like passkeys.

item.title: Block URL obfuscation in the browser with Push
item.description: Push now blocks URL schema obfuscation, countering a common technique used by attackers to bypass URL detections for phishing pages and malicious IPs.

item.title: How the browser became the main cyber battleground
item.description: How attacks have moved away from endpoints and internal networks to the browser — a blind spot for traditional security tools.

item.title: 6 browser-based attacks security teams need to know about
item.description: What security teams need to know about the browser-based attack techniques that are the leading cause of breaches.

item.title: How Push stopped a high risk LinkedIn spear-phishing attack
item.description: How Push saved a company exec from a sophisticated Attacker-in-the-Middle phishing attack delivered via a LinkedIn direct message.

item.title: How cyber breaches are driving tighter MFA requirements
item.description: MFA regulators, insurers, and policy-makers are getting tighter on their MFA requirements, fuelled by public cyber breaches.

item.title: Fixing SecOps alert fatigue with browser telemetry
item.description: How browser data can improve detection fidelity and reduce alert fatigue, enabling SecOps teams to save time and detect more attacks.

item.title: Analyzing the latest Sneaky2FA BITB phishing page
item.description: Analyzing a BITB phishing page linked to the Sneaky2FA Phishing-as-a-Service operation.

item.title: Analysing a malvertising attack targeting Google accounts
item.description: Analysing a malvertising attack targeting Google business accounts that was intercepted by Push.

item.title: Analysing a sophisticated Google malvertising attack
item.description: Push recently detected and blocked a malvertising attack impersonating TradingView designed to hijack Google Workspace accounts.

item.title: ConsentFix: Browser-native ClickFix hijacks OAuth grants
item.description: Analysing "ConsentFix", a new browser-native attack technique we've detected in the wild, combining OAuth consent phishing with a ClickFix-style user prompt.

item.title: Analysing 2025's top phishing trends
item.description: Analysing the key trends that defined phishing attacks in 2025, and what these changes mean for security teams heading into 2026.

item.title: Google Search malvertising campaign impersonating Ahrefs
item.description: New samples linked to a Push-tracked malvertising campaign detected, targeting Google accounts via an Ahrefs lure.

item.title: Analysing the malvertising criminal ecosystem
item.description: Attackers are going out of their way to target Google Ad Manager accounts, powering malvertising scams. Here’s what you need to know.

item.title: ConsentFix debrief: insights, recommendations & predictions
item.description: New insights on the ConsentFix campaign stopped by Push.

item.title: Cyber Essentials 2026: Mandatory MFA on ALL cloud services
item.description: Big changes are being made to the Cyber Essentials scheme in 2026 that will change how companies must validate compliance. Here’s what you need to know.

item.title: Guide: How to manage and block browser extensions using Push
item.description: How to detect risky and malicious extensions and block them from running in employee browsers.

item.title: InstallFix: Weaponizing malvertised install guides
item.description: Attackers are impersonating popular developer tools like Claude Code to distribute fake install instructions via malicious search engine ads.

item.title: Analysing the rise in device code phishing attacks in 2026
item.description: Device code phishing is seeing a huge spike in adoption in 2026, enabling attackers to steal access tokens while bypassing standard access controls.

item.title: Guide: How to use Push to protect users from browser threats
item.description: How to use in-browser controls to stop browser-based attacks before compromise can occur

item.title: Analyzing browser sync attacks and how to stop them
item.description: Browser sync attacks result in business credentials being compromised via personal account and device breaches. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: Investigating a new criminal toolkit for ConsentFix
item.description: Investigating a new criminal toolkit for ConsentFix being promoted on criminal forums.

item.title: What we learned from inside a criminal phishing panel
item.description: We got an inside look at a phishing panel used in criminal campaigns linked to operators like ShinyHunters and BlackFile. Here’s what we found.

item.title: How three techniques are behind ShinyHunters' 2026 campaigns
item.description: ShinyHunters' breach of Instructure is the latest in a long series of attacks. Here's our view of the big picture.

item.title: How we built an agentic threat hunting pipeline at Push
item.description: How we built an end-to-end threat hunting and detection engineering capability at Push that uses AI agents as a force multiplier.

item.title: How to avoid the browser security buyer's trap
item.description: Securing the browser vs. securing the organization via the browser — what's the difference?

item.title: 7 things Omdia's latest report tells us about the SEB market
item.description: Unpacking the latest research report from Omdia and what it means for the secure enterprise browser market.

item.title: 7 things we learned from our conversation with John Hammond
item.description: Here are 7 things we learned from our conversation with John Hammond on the "Why the browser is the new battleground" webinar.

item.title: 7 things we learned from our conversation with Troy Hunt
item.description: Here are 7 things we learned from our conversation with Troy Hunt on the "Yes, you've been pwned" webinar.

item.title: Enterprise browser vs. browser extension solution analysis
item.description: If you're building a shortlist of browser security vendors, do you need a full-stack enterprise browser, or browser security extension?

item.title: 7 things we learned from our conversation with Matt Johansen
item.description: What we learned from sitting down with Matt Johansen to discuss the difference between security theater and security that actually works.

item.title: How browser visibility and control can achieve AI compliance
item.description: AI regulations across the US, EU, and UK are converging on obligations that most organizations can't meet without browser visibility into AI tool use.
